query: Given sales data: Q1=$45000, Q2=$52000, Q3=$48000, Q4=$61000. Calculate the total annual revenue, quarterly growth rates, and identify the best performing quarter.

rubrics.name: total_calculation
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly sum all quarters to get $206,000

rubrics.name: growth_rates
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate quarter-over-quarter growth rates

rubrics.name: analysis
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly identify Q4 as best performing quarter

rubrics.name: presentation
rubrics.rubric: Must present results clearly with proper formatting

query: A company has 3 departments: Engineering (50 employees, avg salary $95000), Sales (30 employees, avg salary $75000), Marketing (20 employees, avg salary $70000). Calculate the company-wide average salary and identify which department has the highest total salary cost.
reference_answer: Company avg: $83500. Highest total cost: Engineering ($4,750,000)

rubrics.name: weighted_average
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate weighted average salary across all departments

rubrics.name: total_cost
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate total salary cost per department (employees * avg salary)

rubrics.name: identification
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly identify Engineering as highest total cost department

rubrics.name: explanation
rubrics.rubric: Must explain the calculation methodology

query: Given monthly revenue data: Jan=$12000, Feb=$15000, Mar=$18000, Apr=$14000, May=$22000, Jun=$25000. Calculate the 3-month moving average for each applicable month and identify any significant trends.
reference_answer: 3-month MA: Mar=$15000, Apr=$15667, May=$18000, Jun=$20333. Trend: Strong growth in May-Jun

rubrics.name: moving_average
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate 3-month moving average for Mar through Jun

rubrics.name: trend_analysis
rubrics.rubric: Must identify the growth trend, especially the acceleration in May-Jun

rubrics.name: interpretation
rubrics.rubric: Must provide meaningful business interpretation of the trends

rubrics.name: presentation
rubrics.rubric: Must present results in a clear, organized format

query: A/B test results: Group A (1000 users, 50 conversions), Group B (1000 users, 65 conversions). Calculate conversion rates, the relative improvement, and determine if this is statistically significant at 95% confidence.
reference_answer: A: 5.0%, B: 6.5%. Improvement: 30%. Statistically significant? Need chi-square or z-test

rubrics.name: conversion_rates
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate conversion rates: A=5.0%, B=6.5%

rubrics.name: improvement
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate relative improvement: (6.5-5.0)/5.0 = 30%

rubrics.name: statistical_test
rubrics.rubric: Must apply appropriate statistical test (z-test for proportions or chi-square)

rubrics.name: interpretation
rubrics.rubric: Must clearly state whether result is statistically significant and what it means

query: Given customer churn data: Month 1: 1000 customers, Month 2: 950, Month 3: 910, Month 4: 870, Month 5: 840. Calculate monthly churn rates and predict Month 6 customers using the average churn rate.

rubrics.name: churn_rates
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate monthly churn rates from the data

rubrics.name: average_churn
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly calculate the average churn rate

rubrics.name: prediction
rubrics.rubric: Must apply average churn rate to predict Month 6 customers

rubrics.name: analysis
rubrics.rubric: Should note that churn rate is decreasing (positive trend)
